runway.readiness_blocker: Runway account or credit readiness is not sufficient for a successful Gen-4.5 canary.
runway.strategy_implication: Runway should not receive another live submit until credits/account readiness is resolved and a fresh exact authorization is provided.

runninghub.provider_error_meaning: Standard Model API is restricted to Enterprise-Shared API Keys only.
runninghub.readiness_blocker: The selected RunningHub Standard Model API route requires an Enterprise-Shared API Key or an alternate authorized API path.
runninghub.strategy_implication: The local adapter and duration contract are close enough to preserve, but provider access must be changed before any further live submit.

decision_options.description: Apply for or configure a RunningHub Enterprise-Shared API Key for the current Standard Model API route.
why: R3-8M reached RunningHub upload and submit with exactly one upload call and one submit call.
The remaining RunningHub blocker is provider account/key type, not local duration mapping or image artifact readiness.
This preserves the already-tested upload-first standard model route and avoids introducing a new provider contract.
required_before_live_retry: Human/operator confirms the RunningHub account has an Enterprise-Shared API Key authorized for Standard Model API.
Credential is configured outside this task boundary without printing or committing secret values.
Run local env/preflight checks without exposing secret values.
Run a fresh dry-run guard report against the exact 6 second contract.
Obtain a new exact user authorization phrase for any live upload/submit.

decision_options.description: Switch to an authorized RunningHub non-standard-model or workflow API path that the current account is allowed to use.
why: This is the best fallback if Enterprise-Shared API Key access is not available.
It keeps the RunningHub direction while avoiding the Standard Model API key-type restriction.
It will need a new contract-freeze and dry-run before any live call because endpoints, request fields, upload semantics, and output receipts may differ.
required_before_live_retry: Identify the authorized RunningHub route without reading or printing credentials.
Add offline adapter tests for request shape, duration, aspect ratio, upload/output receipts, and fail-closed behavior.
Generate a dry-run report showing no network call attempted.
Obtain a new exact user authorization phrase for the first live canary on the new route.

decision_options.description: Return to Runway only after credits/account readiness is resolved.
why: The real storyboard keyframe Runway canary reached the provider boundary and failed for insufficient credits.
Another Runway submit before account readiness would likely repeat the same paid-call boundary failure.
Runway remains viable only after the account/credits state changes and a new exact authorization is provided.
required_before_live_retry: Run env/preflight locally without printing secrets.
Refresh the canary dry-run guard for model gen4.5, duration 2, and ratio 720:1280.
Obtain a new exact user authorization phrase for a single-submit live canary.

decision_options.description: Add a third provider path only if it is lower-risk and can be contract-frozen before live use.
why: A third provider adds a fresh contract surface, new credential boundary, new failure modes, and new receipt requirements.
It is justified only if both Runway account readiness and RunningHub access qualification are slower or riskier than integrating a third provider.
Any third provider must start with contract freeze, offline adapter tests, and dry-run reports before a live canary.
required_before_live_retry: Freeze request, auth, duration, aspect ratio, output, and receipt contract.
Add offline tests and secret redaction checks.
Obtain a new exact user authorization phrase for any live call.

recommendation.decision: Use RunningHub as the next provider-access focus, but do not submit again on the Standard Model API route until Enterprise-Shared API Key access is confirmed. If that access cannot be obtained, switch to an authorized RunningHub workflow or non-standard-model API route and re-freeze the contract offline before live use.

human_selection.selection_text: 选择 RunningHub Enterprise-Shared API Key 路径作为主路径。
next_safe_preparation: Confirm outside Codex that the RunningHub account/API key is Enterprise-Shared and authorized for Standard Model API.
After the credential is configured by the human/operator, run local env/preflight checks without printing secret values.
Generate a fresh no-network dry-run guard report for the 6 second RunningHub Standard Model API route.
Use a new exact current user authorization phrase before any live upload or submit.

next_step.recommended_follow_up: Prepare RunningHub Enterprise-Shared API Key readiness only after human/operator confirms the key type and provides any needed credential-read authorization.
next_step.requires_user_decision: DONE: Jenn selected RunningHub Enterprise-Shared API Key as the primary path.
